chief_complaint: A 36-year-old female patient presented with progressive weakness, lower cranial nerve involvement, and areflexia.
history: The patient initially presented with symptoms of infection 10 days before admission, including oropharyngeal pain and diarrhea. She was later diagnosed with Guillain-Barre syndrome (GBS) with associated cardiovascular dysautonomia. Echocardiography revealed apical hypokinesia of the left ventricle, consistent with Takotsubo cardiomyopathy (TCM). Additionally, her thyroid profile indicated hyperthyroidism, fulfilling the criteria for a thyroid storm.
exam: Sinus tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, and apical hypokinesia of the left ventricle were observed.
labs: Diagnostic evaluation included echocardiography showing apical hypokinesia of the left ventricle, thyroid profile indicating hyperthyroidism, and electrocardiogram (ECG) showing sinus tachycardia and atrial fibrillation.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy due to Guillain-Barre Syndrome and Thyroid Storm
differentials: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy due to Guillain-Barre Syndrome and Thyroid Storm
correct_ranking: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy due to Guillain-Barre Syndrome and Thyroid Storm
